source_intake_record.raw_question_text: A person looking into an empty container as shown in figure. When the container is completely filled with a fluid of index of refraction \( n \) and viewed from the same angle, the person can see the center of a coin at the middle of the container’s bottom For what range of values of \( n \) will the center of the coin not be visible for any values of \( h \) and \( d \)?

image_dependency: question explicitly says 'as shown in the figure'
the setup references viewing angle and geometric quantities h and d that are likely defined by the diagram
multi_step: problem combines optical refraction with geometric visibility conditions
question asks for a range of n for which the coin center is not visible for any values of h and d, implying condition derivation rather than direct lookup
wording suggests at least two reasoning stages: relate viewing geometry to refraction, then determine the threshold/range on n
multiple-choice structure with symbolic answer indicates a derived conceptual result
verifiability: gold answer '(n>2)' is precise and directly comparable
choices are provided and one option exactly matches the answer

decision_reasons: question, answer, and required image asset are all present
asset registry passed with no recorded issues
initial scores are strong: high image dependency, good multi-step reasoning, and high verifiability
the only noted risk is that image content was not directly inspected, but this is not enough to outweigh the otherwise complete and high-quality asset set
